mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the federal funds rate raise the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing S&P 500 valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of periods following a 25bps rate hike, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate below 4.0% signals extreme labor market tightness that boosts corporate revenue expectations, driving positive 5-day S&P 500 returns as markets price in sustained economic expansion.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of instances when UNRATE crosses below 4.0%, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.01 in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts indicates a broad-based contraction in consumer demand, which reduces near-term corporate earnings forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST are followed by positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by >0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, an increase in the industrial production index signals resilient supply-side capacity that offsets rate hike headwinds, leading to positive 20-day S&P 500 returns as markets revise recession probabilities downward.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening cycles are followed by negative 20-day returns in >65% of observations, or the signal predictive accuracy vanishes when FEDFUNDS slope is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 200.0, a concurrent rise in UNRATE creates a stagflationary signal that erodes real corporate margins and forces aggressive central bank tightening, predicting negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Stagflationary combinations yield positive 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant with p > 0.10 in multivariate regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with aggregate corporate profit growth, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices that manifests as positive 5-day forward returns following data releases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns with t-stat < 1.5 over any 5-year rolling window, or returns are negative in >60% of positive-GDP-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive monthly declines in total nonfarm payrolls confirm a labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, causing institutional deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >50% of periods following three consecutive PAYEMS declines, or the signal fails to predict return direction with >55% accuracy in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized expansion in industrial production and retail sales during a post-recession expansion phase signals strong domestic demand absorption, driving positive 20-day S&P 500 returns as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Synchronized increases in INDPRO and RSAFS are followed by negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the joint signal adds no incremental predictive power over single-series models with AIC difference < 2.
